evidence-binding: n1-residue-fixes 9e9683c09f7c

RED: (1) `node scripts/test-sink-merge.js` (pre-producer-edit, worktree @ run base) — 3 failed, 102 passed; failing signature: `FAIL: #715 k: the sibling receipt must NOT be listed in foreign_dirt; got ["kaola-workflow/archive/sibling-71591/.cache/sink-receipt.json"]` (+ `#715 k: sink must exit 0 ... got 1`, `#715 k: status must be sinked; got undefined`) — an untracked sibling archive receipt (`kaola-workflow/archive/<sibling>/.cache/sink-receipt.json`, mid-cycle steps) was classified bucket-3 foreign dirt and refused the test project's `--sink` with `sink_blocked`. (2) `node scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js --only testSinkForeignDirtExemptsSiblingReceipt715` — `Error: #715: the sibling interrupted-sink receipt must NOT appear in foreign_dirt, got: ["kaola-workflow/archive/sibling-7159/.cache/sink-receipt.json","kaola-workflow/other-project/workflow-state.md"]` (exit non-zero). (3) `node scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js --only testReleaseFromLinkedWorktreeCleansMainCopy` — `Error: #715: release must report discard_archive_committed:true, got: {"released":true,...,"archived":true,"dest":".../kaola-workflow/archive/issue-703.discarded-2026-07-18T06-29-41-503Z",...}` — release archived the `.discarded-` dest but never committed it (field absent), the #715(a) repro. Guards passing pre-edit as designed: `#715 l` (over-exemption: sibling non-receipt + look-alikes stay foreign dirt) and `#715 m` (#518 own-receipt regression lock) were green both before and after; all 102 pre-existing test-sink-merge assertions stayed green.
<!-- GREEN: paste GREEN here -->
GREEN: the three RED signatures now pass, with every pre-existing assertion unchanged-green. `node scripts/test-sink-merge.js` → `Sink-merge (#694/#700/#705/#707/#711/#715) test suite passed: 105 assertions.` (exit 0; was 3 failed / 102 passed pre-edit — the #715 k/l/m scenarios add 3 passing tests: sibling archive receipt exempt + byte-untouched (k), over-exemption guard (l), #518 own-receipt regression lock (m)). `node scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` (FULL run, final tree) → `Workflow walkthrough simulation passed` (exit 0), including the extended `testReleaseFromLinkedWorktreeCleansMainCopy` (release now reports `discard_archive_committed:true`, no `.discarded-` path in `git status --porcelain`, the dest is a tree at HEAD, and the follow-up sink for issue-704 reaches `status:sinked` with no manual commit) and the new `testSinkForeignDirtExemptsSiblingReceipt715` (sibling receipt absent from `foreign_dirt`, genuine foreign file still listed, zero mutation). Rest of the GREEN chain, all exit 0: `node scripts/test-claim-hardening.js` → `claim-hardening tests passed (450 assertions)`; `node scripts/validate-script-sync.js` → `OK: 25 common scripts, 28 byte-identical groups, 8 rename-normalized families, 2 hooks.json families (config + hooks dir), and 7 forge export-superset families in sync.`; `node scripts/edition-sync.js --check` → `edition-sync: 12 forge aggregator ports, 25 COMMON_SCRIPTS mirrors, and 28 byte-identical groups in parity with canonical.`; standalone suites: `node plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/test-gitlab-sinks.js` → `GitLab sink tests passed`; `node plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/test-gitea-sinks.js` → `Gitea sink tests passed`; `node scripts/test-kimi-edition.js` → `kimi-edition test passed (577 assertions).`; `node scripts/test-opencode-edition.js` → `opencode-edition test passed (547 assertions).`

## What changed (one semantic change, four trees)

Fix (a) — release/watch-pr commits its discard archive. New shared helper `commitDiscardArchive(result, project)` beside `archiveProjectDirSafely` in `scripts/kaola-workflow-claim.js:2311`: stages the ACTUAL `result.dest` (never a reconstructed plain path — the #700 collision-suffix lesson) via `git -C <dest> rev-parse --show-toplevel` + `git add -A -- <rel>`, skips the commit on the diff-quiet guard, commits `chore: discard archive <project>` scoped to the dest pathspec, then verifies the archive is a tree at HEAD. Called from BOTH `.discarded-` sites: `cmdRelease` (`scripts/kaola-workflow-claim.js:3326`, placed AFTER the in-place branch restore so the commit lands on the restored base branch — committing before the checkout+delete would strand it on the deleted feature branch) and the watch-pr CLOSED sweep (`scripts/kaola-workflow-claim.js:4296`). Outcome recorded on the emitted JSON: `discard_archive_committed: true|false` plus `discard_archive_commit_detail` on failure (cmdRelease also pushes a loud `warnings[]` entry); a failed commit never throws past the emit and never strands the release. Local git only — `KAOLA_WORKFLOW_OFFLINE` does not skip it (proven: the walkthrough release scenario runs OFFLINE=1 and asserts the commit at HEAD).

Fix (b) — any-project receipt exemption. `sinkPreflight` (`scripts/kaola-workflow-sink-merge.js:1178`) now matches the EXACT path `/^kaola-workflow\/(?:archive\/)?[^/]+\/\.cache\/sink-receipt\.json$/` instead of a this-project-keyed Set — `<seg>` is exactly one path segment, any project live or archived, unconditional across porcelain statuses. Look-alikes (`sink-receipt.json.tmp`, nested `x/.cache/sink-receipt.json`, a `sink-receipt.json` directory / trailing-slash form) and any other sibling-tree file stay bucket-3 foreign dirt; `sink-fallback.json` stays unexempted (out of scope per the issue). The #518 comment records the sibling rationale: classification-only — the sink never stages/touches/mutates the sibling receipt (the byte-untouched assertion in #715 k proves it).

Propagation: codex twins `plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/kaola-workflow-{claim,sink-merge}.js` byte-replicated from canonical (verified byte-identical to canonical at the run base; `edition-sync.js --check` parity green). Hand-mirrored every hunk into `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/kaola-gitlab-workflow-{claim,sink-merge}.js` and `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/kaola-gitea-workflow-{claim,sink-merge}.js` modulo forge nouns (helper comment references each port's own sink-merge filename). No new module.exports in any tree (helper is file-local), so the forge export-superset invariant is untouched — `validate-script-sync.js` green.

## Deviations / notes

- test-sink-merge.js scenario (m) fixture initially planted receipts with `preflight:'done'`, which skipped the preflight scan on resume; corrected to all-`pending` steps before the recorded RED run above (the recorded RED is the corrected fixture).
- The walkthrough release scenario's follow-up sink initially refused on the fixture's own untracked `bin/gh.js` gh-shim; the scenario now removes the shim dir before the sink leg (fixture hygiene, not production behavior). The release spawn env additionally gained `GIT_ISOLATION_ENV` (additive) so the discard commit can't pick up a user's global git config.
- One ordering refinement beyond the verbatim plan text: the `cmdRelease` commit call was placed AFTER the in-place branch-restore block (the plan named only the two call sites, not intra-function ordering) — required for correctness, since committing before the checkout+delete would strand the archive commit on the soon-to-be-deleted feature branch. Documented in the code comment.
- Tie-breaker derivation (First Principles, axiom 1 correct-first): staging ONLY `result.dest` (per plan) means a branchless in-place run whose live folder was TRACKED on main still leaves `D` deletions uncommitted after release; that shape is outside the issue's repro (untracked main-root residue) and outside the plan's declared seam, so it was not widened.
